2005.text: Can one conversation change the world? Quick and direct access to information can deliver news from abroad, encourage a child's curiosity, and reunite long-lost friends. It can also produce ideas that transform how people live their lives. At Google, our strategy is simple: we hire great people and encourage them to make their dreams a reality. We believe in hard work, a fun atmosphere, and the so

Looking for an adventure? Google's approach is to foster creativity and cooperation in the pursuit of a common goal: creating the world's best search service . In doing so, we have developed strong revenue streams enabling us to attain profitability in a competitive marketplace. 2008.text: Chances are you have a good idea of where you want to go in life. At Google, we've designed a culture that helps you get there. From our flexible, project-based approach to corporate structure to our innovative perks and benefits, we do everything we can to make sure our employees not only have great jobs, but great lives. Into being challenged? Into having fun? Want to change the world? If the an
2008.heading: Let's work together.

2009.text: At Google, we understand that our worldwide success results from our globally diverse workforce. In every Google office, you will find challenging projects and smart people with potential to change the world. Googlers relish the freedom to create the next generation of web technologies in an environment designed to foster collaboration, creativity, health, and happiness. What’s it like to work at
2009.heading: Let’s work together.

2010.text: At Google, our world is flat. When everyone in the company is reaching out, not up, good ideas can go a lot farther.
2010.heading: Do more. A lot more.

2010.heading: Let’s work together.

2011.text: At Google, innovation and creativity keeps our projects changing and improving. Our consistency comes from our Googlers – smart, amazing people who foster an environment of collaboration and fun.
2011.heading: Let’s work together.

2012.text: Get to know who we are - from the inside out. From our history and culture, to the people behind our products, get the inside scoop on all things Googley. Take me to Life at Google At Google, we don’t just accept difference - we celebrate it, we support it, and we thrive on it for the benefit of our employees, our products and our community. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and
2012.heading: Life at Google

2012.text: You can be serious without a suit. Our founders built Google around the idea that work should be challenging, and the challenge should be fun. We believe that great, creative things are more likely to happen with the right company culture–and that doesn’t just mean lava lamps and rubber balls. There is an emphasis on team achievements and pride in individual accomplishments that contribute to our
2012.heading: Ten things we know to be true

2012.text: Google is not a conventional company, and we don’t intend to become one. True, we share attributes with the world’s most successful organizations – a focus on innovation and smart business practices comes to mind – but even as we continue to grow, we’re committed to retaining a small-company feel. At Google, we know that every employee has something important to say, and that every employee is int

2014.heading: Employee networks at Google

2014.text: We’ve always wanted Google to be a place that brought together smart, talented people from a diversity of backgrounds, and where you could bring your whole self to work. Lots has been written about our great perks, but read on to find out what our culture is really all about.
2014.heading: Culture and Diversity

2014.text: People are at the core of who we are, and Googlers have taken all kinds of paths in their careers to get here. Learn more about what we do and some of the choices we’ve made along the way.
2014.heading: Googler's Voice

2015.heading: Culture and Diversity

2015.text: People are at the core of who we are, and Googlers have taken all kinds of paths in their careers to get here. Learn more about what we do and some of the choices we’ve made along the way.

2015.text: Get to know who we are - from the inside out. From our history and culture, to the people behind our products, get the inside scoop on all things Googley. Take me to Life at Google Still a student? This way to student jobs and internships. At Google, we don’t just accept difference - we celebrate it, we support it, and we thrive on it for the benefit of our employees, our products and our communit
2015.heading: Life at Google

2015.text: Whether it’s creating self-driving cars or translating the web into 64 languages, we love big bets. Our company culture encourages experimentation and the free flow of ideas. This perspective is not limited to our engineering teams and technical roles, but extends to all Googlers who share a desire to challenge what’s possible.
2015.heading: Thinking big: Larry Page talks moon shots

2015.text: Get to know who we are - from the inside out. From our history and culture, to the people behind our products, get the inside scoop on all things Googley.
2015.heading: Life at Google

2016.text: Get to know who we are - from the inside out. From our history and culture, to the people behind our products, get the inside scoop on all things Googley. Take me to Life at Google Still a student? This way to student jobs and internships. At Google, we don’t just accept difference - we celebrate it, we support it, and we thrive on it for the benefit of our employees, our products and our communit
2016.heading: Life at Google

2016.text: We value giving back to the community just as much as we value the products we create. We match Googlers’ charitable donations and add donations for hours Googlers work as volunteers.
2016.heading: Give back

2016.text: Your family matters to you, so they’re important to us, too. Many of our benefit programs and onsite amenities are aimed at supporting you and your loved ones through life’s various stages—we offer generous parental leave policies, retirement savings plans, death benefits, and much more. In some of our offices, you can even bring your canine family members to work!
2016.heading: Support your loved ones

2016.text: Lifelong learning is inherently Googley. That’s why we offer extensive opportunities for personal and professional development. Whether it’s onsite coding or cooking classes, degree programs, or the guitar lessons you’ve been meaning to take, we’ll support you in doing what you love.
2016.heading: Invest in you

2016.text: From our highly-rated retirement savings matches to financial advisors and planning services, we offer many resources to help keep you financially fit.
2016.heading: Manage your finances

2017.text: Google should be a place where people from different backgrounds and experiences come to do their best work–a place where every Googler feels they belong. The truth is that we’re not there yet. We know diversity and inclusion are values critical to our success and future innovation. We also know challenging bias–inside and outside our walls–is the right thing to do. That’s why we continue to suppo
2017.heading: Building a Google that works for everyone

2017.text: We’re ensuring Google continues to be a fair and inclusive place to work for everyone.
2017.heading: Inclusion

2017.text: “A diverse mix of voices leads to better discussions, decisions, and outcomes for everyone.”
We're expanding the ways we find and hire our next Googlers.
2017.heading: Hiring

2017.text: We’re still not where we want to be when it comes to diversity, but last year, we made progress in our efforts to build a more diverse Google. We’re hiring above our current representation of Blacks and Hispanics, and we increased representation of women in leadership and in technical roles. Of new hires in 2015 were Black, compared to 2% of our current population. Of new hires in 2015 were Hispan
2017.heading: In 2015, we moved in the right direction.

2023.text: Large or small, each one of our offices is designed to inspire innovation, big ideas, and community.
2023.heading: Our locations

2023.text: Together, we create access to information and build products for everyone. Want to be a Googler? Find your team. Develop the products and tools of the future for billions of users. Equip businesses with the right tools to help them grow. Find, grow, and support the people who make Google great. Make people's lives better through technology.
2023.heading: Find your team

2024.text: Large or small, each one of our offices is designed to inspire innovation, big ideas, and community.

Equip businesses with the right tools to help them grow. Find, grow, and support the people who make Google great. Make people's lives better through technology.
2024.heading: Find your team

2024.text: Whether it's business or engineering & technology, we're here to help get you to your future.
2024.heading: Build your future with Google

2024.text: Scholarships Google offers a range of scholarships for high-school students pursuing a post-secondary education in technology. Explore Internships Our #GoogleInterns and residents help build products that create opportunities for everyone. Bring your insight, imagination, and a healthy disregard for the impossible. Explore Apprenticeships Apprenticeships join different teams to gain practical skil
2024.heading: Discover the opportunities

2025.text: We’re always working to ensure our systems support everyone and our managers are equipped to help every employee thrive.
2025.heading: 3 Advancing fairness in everything we do

2025.text: We offer generous benefits and targeted programs to support the whole person, no matter who you are.
2025.heading: 1 Offering tools for wellbeing and growth

2025.text: We’re designing with communities that are often overlooked so that what we build is truly helpful for everyone.
2025.heading: Building technology, together

2025.text: We work as Team Google - one mission-driven team that solves meaningful challenges for our users while cheering each other on along the way.
2025.heading: 2 Strengthening our culture of bold and responsible innovation

2025.text: We’re working with experts and researchers with deep expertise and cultural context of the communities we’re designing for.
2025.heading: Driving groundbreaking research

2012.text: Great just isn’t good enough. We see being great at something as a starting point, not an endpoint. We set ourselves goals we know we can’t reach yet, because we know that by stretching to meet them we can get further than we expected. 2014.text: People are at the core of who we are, and Googlers have taken all kinds of paths in their careers to get here. Learn more about what we do and some of the choices we’ve made along the way.
2014.heading: Googler's Voice

2014.text: We’ve always wanted Google to be a place that brought together smart, talented people from a diversity of backgrounds, and where you could bring your whole self to work. Lots has been written about our great perks, but read on to find out what our culture is really all about.
2014.heading: Culture and Diversity

2014.text: Diversity is a core part of our business and who we are. Watch this clip ...
2014.heading: Employee networks at Google

2014.text: Googlers solve complex problems everyday in the name of our core mission to organize the world’s information and make it universally accessible to our users. But what makes working at Google truly unique is the workplace culture that encourages innovation and a healthy disregard for the impossible. Watch Alan Eustace, SVP of knowledge, explain the secret ingredient that makes Google a great place
2014.heading: Do Cool Things That Matter

2014.text: Since the beginning, we’ve focused on providing the best user experience possible. Whether we’re designing a new Internet browser or a new tweak to the look of the homepage, we take great care to ensure that they will ultimately serve you, rather than our own internal goal or bottom line.
2014.heading: #1: Focus on the user and all else will follow.

2017.text: We're expanding the ways we find and hire our next Googlers.
2017.heading: Hiring

2017.text: There’s no one kind of Googler, so we’re always looking for people who can bring new perspectives and life experiences to our teams. If you’re looking for a place that values your curiosity, passion, and desire to learn, if you’re seeking colleagues who are big thinkers eager to take on fresh challenges as a team, then you’re a future Googler.
2017.heading: How we hire

stanceMinQuote.text: At Google, our strategy is simple: we hire great people and encourage them to make their dreams a reality.
stanceMinQuote.heading: Search openings

stanceMaxQuote.text: At Google, we understand that our worldwide success results from our globally diverse workforce. In every Google office, you will find challenging projects and smart people with potential to change the world. Googlers relish the freedom to create the next generation of web technologies in an environment designed to foster collaboration, creativity, health, and happiness. What’s it like to work at
stanceMaxQuote.heading: Let’s work together.

stanceMinQuote.text: At Google, our world is flat. When everyone in the company is reaching out, not up, good ideas can go a lot farther.
stanceMinQuote.heading: Do more. A lot more.

stanceMaxQuote.text: At Google, innovation and creativity keeps our projects changing and improving. Our consistency comes from our Googlers – smart, amazing people who foster an environment of collaboration and fun.
stanceMaxQuote.heading: Let’s work together.

stanceMaxQuote.heading: Life at Google

stanceMinQuote.text: Fast is better than slow. We know your time is valuable, so when you’re seeking an answer on the web you want it right away–and we aim to please. We may be the only people in the world who can say our goal is to have people leave our website as quickly as possible. By shaving excess bits and bytes from our pages and increasing the efficiency of our serving environment, we’ve broken our own speed r
stanceMinQuote.heading: Ten things we know to be true

stanceMaxQuote.text: We’re ensuring Google continues to be a fair and inclusive place to work for everyone.
stanceMaxQuote.heading: Inclusion

stanceMinQuote.text: There’s no one kind of Googler, so we’re always looking for people who can bring new perspectives and life experiences to our teams. If you’re looking for a place that values your curiosity, passion, and desire to learn, if you’re seeking colleagues who are big thinkers eager to take on fresh challenges as a team, then you’re a future Googler.
stanceMinQuote.heading: How we hire

stanceMaxQuote.text: BOLD Immersion provides a glimpse into the business side of the technology industry and exposure to non-technical career opportunities at Google.
stanceMaxQuote.heading: BOLD Immersion

stanceMinQuote.text: Offering free training and tools, Grow with Google helps you grow your skills, career, or business. Explore programs and register for an in-person workshop.
stanceMinQuote.heading: Grow with Google

stanceMaxQuote.text: Together, we create access to information and build products for everyone. Want to be a Googler? Find your team. Develop the products and tools of the future for billions of users. Equip businesses with the right tools to help them grow. Find, grow, and support the people who make Google great. Make people's lives better through technology.

stanceMinQuote.text: Whether it's business or engineering & technology, we're here to help get you to your future.
stanceMinQuote.heading: Build your future with Google

stanceMaxQuote.text: We’re building tools that make it easier to support businesses that represent and support a range of different communities. Business owners in the U.S. can add attributes to their Business Profiles that customers can see in their listings on Google Search and Google Maps. So people can see if businesses are veteran-owned , women-owned , disabled-owned , LGBTQ+ owned , Asian-owned , Black-owned , L
stanceMaxQuote.heading: Supporting community businesses

stanceMinQuote.text: Every year, 250,000 service members transition out of the military. United States service members can now enter their military occupation code (MOS, AFSC, NEC, etc.) when they search “jobs for Veterans” and find relevant jobs that align with their military experience — making the civilian career shift easier.
stanceMinQuote.heading: Helping Veterans
